scope.summary: Implement the kernel-side trim Glue/Tack placement handler that accepts a panel UV anchor, resolves the nearest cloth particle, constructs a TackDefinitionV1 array (length 1 = Glue; length >= 2 = Tack), writes to tailor_trim_placements, and emits TailorTrimPlaced
acceptance_criteria: A single-click Glue placement produces a tailor_trim_placements row whose tacks_json is a TackDefinitionV1 array of exactly length 1, per TR-6.3.2
A multi-point Tack placement produces a TackDefinitionV1 array of length >= 2
Panel UV coordinates are resolved to the nearest GpuParticle index at load time; particle_uv stored verbatim on TackDefinitionV1
TailorTrimPlaced EventLedger event is emitted on every successful placement write
Placement rows are loadable by the solver as GpuTackConstraint entries without any additional round-trip
Placement is rejected (400-class API error) if trim_id does not exist in tailor_trims
scope.risk_if_missed: Models calling place_trim MCP tool (MT-263) get a placement row that the solver cannot consume correctly because the Glue vs Tack count distinction is never enforced; TR-6.3.2 compliance is broken silently

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
